observations.recommendation:
Management ensure that the Chairperson of the Inventory Committee:

Fully implement the procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;


Ensure that  a complete physical count of all PPEs is conducted by assigning the appropriate personnel; and 

Submit the report of the physical count after reconciliation with the accounting records to the Audit Team in order to validate Management’s assertions on the recorded balances of the PPE accounts.

Management should:

Establish a General Services Office or designate a GSO Officer for the control and management of supplies who shall be responsible for the conduct of the physical inventory of all supplies and materials in accordance with existing rules and regulations to verify the accuracy of reported balances and the submission of a report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

Require the designated supply officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective records for the latter to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances.
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of their respective records, identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books.

Municipal Treasurer should:

Revise the abstract/ summary of collection to include the 15 per cent share to be held in trust for OBO operations;

Establish the balances of the 15 per cent share of the OBO for CY 2023 and prior years and transfer the same to its TF bank account. 

The Municipal Accountant should:

Make the necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous recording of the 15 per cent share of the OBO from building permit fees and other charges in CY 2023, as follows:

GF Books:
Dr. Prior Period Adj. ₱110,385.39
Cr. Trust Liabilities           ₱110,385.39

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to determine the balances of 15 per cent share of the OBO in the prior years’, that is from the effectivity date of the DPWH-DILG JMC No. 001 dated July 04, 2013 up to CY 2022. The same pro-forma journal entry will be used as shown above.

Recognize only 80 per cent of the collections from building permit fees and other charges as Permit Fees and record the 15 per cent share of the OBO as Trust Liabilities in the GF books. A separate Subsidiary Ledger is to be maintained for this purpose/ fund.

Management should:

Monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF so that the programs, projects and activities strengthening the Municipality’s capacity for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented; 

Evaluate the projects funded under the continuing appropriations for LDRRMF taking into consideration the priorities, needs and desired outcomes of the Municipality and its constituents and consider the re-alignment of appropriations when deemed necessary; and

Prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and observe the timely submission thereof.

Municipal Accountant should:

Facilitate the preparation and the filing of request for write-off for dormant accounts with remote/futile collectability following the documentary requirements and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008, amending COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

Pending the submission of request for write-off, provide an allowance for impairment of the Other Receivables account.

Municipal Mayor to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; 

Furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the certified list to serve as the basis for recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables based on the list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer in order to record in the books accurate and verifiable data pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1.

Management submit a written justification for the noted deficiency.  




Henceforth, strictly observe the policies and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advance.

Management should:

Submit an amended LDRRMFIP that includes the list of projects and activities charged against the unexpended LDRRMF in the previous years in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and



Submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and henceforth, observe the timely submission thereof.

The Municipal Mayor direct the Chairperson of the Inventory and Disposal Committee to initiate the disposal of the unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 379 of R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular 89-296.

The Management to direct the Chairperson of the Inventory and Disposal Committee to send letter of demand to those AOs for the production of the missing properties. Otherwise, if accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE could not be pinpointed after investigation, authority for de-recognition thereof from the books of accounts may be requested from the COA as prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

The Municipal Mayor and the BAC submit a valid written justification and the documents mentioned as lacking to prevent disallowance in audit. 

Also, the concerned officials refund the amount of ₱13,200.00 representing overpayment in the procurement of air purifier and irradiation lamp. 

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer should check the accuracy of the amount to be paid and the completeness of the attached documents before payment is made to avoid occurrence of the same nature.
The Management return the unspent fund of ₱1 million from Senator Alan Peter Cayetano to the National Treasury so that it can be used for other priority projects of the government.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to:
hasten the completion of the physical count of its properties including real property accounts and conduct reconciliation thereof;
initiate the preparation and maintenance of the required PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each category of PPE for monitoring and reconciliation purposes; and
prioritize compliance with the preliminary activities required under COA Circular 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts to establish the cost of the actual and existing PPE items owned by the municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to trace and analyze the variances and thereafter, make necessary adjustment in their respective books of accounts; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to correct the balance of the closed current account of LBP- Cebu City.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer:
speed up the update of the RPTAR/Taxpayers’ Index Cards in order to generate a certified list as basis of the RPT and SET receivables in the accounting records; and
coordinate with the Provincial Government for the computerized system of the Real Property Tax that they have provided to other Municipalities in monitoring real property taxes.

We recommended and Municipal Mayor agreed to require:
the GSO, Inventory Committee, and Accounting Office to hasten the completion of the physical inventory of supplies and materials in accordance with existing rules and regulations to verify the accuracy of reported balances and submit the required report thereon to the Auditor for evaluation; and
the GSO and Accounting Office prepare and maintain the stock cards and supplies ledger cards, respectively, for monitoring and reconciliation purposes.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to review and prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and to take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the supporting documents of these payable accounts and send a letter of confirmation to the creditors to determine the validity of the recorded balances. Consequently, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for unsupported payables to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace and prepare the necessary adjustment on the discrepancy noted in order to present the correct balance of the unexpended balance of the DRRMF.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the Municipal Engineering Office and MPDC, as well as other concerned Offices, such as the end-users, coordinate and reconcile their respective records, collectively formulate a comprehensive yet realistic plan to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20 per cent DF and ensure that programs and projects incorporated in the Plan are all “Implementation Ready” to guarantee the prompt accomplishment of the same.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
MPDC, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials:
a.1 evaluate the capacity of the LGU to implement the projects/programs funded by the other government agencies. Otherwise, return the funds to the respective SAs.
a.2 reconcile their respective records, conduct regular monitoring to ensure that fund transfers are implemented and liquidated within the prescribed period;
Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials return or seek authority from the source agencies to realign the other excess/unspent balances of the fully implemented projects; and
Municipal Accountant analyze the accounts with negative balances and identify the causes thereon and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances.

We recommended and the Management through the LDRRM Officer agreed to prepare an LDRRMFIP that includes projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of the previous years and ensure utilization thereof in conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
send a demand letter to the concerned Accountable Officers for the liquidation/settlement of their outstanding cash advances especially to those who are still connected with the Municipality including the withholding of their salary pursuant to Section 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
initiate the request for authority to write-off from COA for the accounts outstanding more than 10 years, if collectability could no longer be ascertained, following the procedures and requirements provided under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MRHMO Assistant to facilitate the hiring of qualified personnel for the LDRRM Officer position as well as create and fill up the three (3) DRRM Office Staff positions to assist in the discharge of the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRMO in accordance with JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and the concerned Municipal Treasurers agreed to coordinate with the BLGF and DOF and submit a written explanation supported with legal basis on the grant of additional RATA, for evaluation by the Audit Team.
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and MHRMO Assistant agreed to organize an IASO in compliance with DBM AO No. 70, NGICS and Section 124 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal GAD Focal Person and other department heads agreed to ensure that the PPAs identified in the GAD Plan and Budget be implemented by the respective offices concerned to mitigate any gender issues present in the locality and achieve gender equality and women empowerment.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Payroll In-charge and Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices to conduct a reconciliation of the said accounts to reflect the correct account balances, and to remit intact the amounts withheld monthly to BIR, GSIS, PAG-IBIG, and PhilHealth to avoid paying the penalties, surcharges, and interests.

We recommended and Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MHRMO to prepare a Job Order Contract detailing the individual daily rate, assigned offices/department, period of service and statement of duties and responsibilities to be duly signed by both the hired JO worker and the LGU.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the inclusions in the reciprocal accounts and examine if it is necessary to remain as an Intra- Agency Receivable or as an Intra-Agency Payable, and make the necessary adjustments if needed to be able to reflect correct and accurate balances as at a given date.

RHU make a written representation addressed to the CPHO to clarify the issuance of vaccines that were supported with an unsigned RIS as well as the unit cost of the unused 192 and 12 vials of Sinovac and Sinopharm vaccines, respectively so that these could be recorded and fairly valued in the books of the Municipality as inventory as at December 31, 2022 in addition to the nine remaining Pfizer vaccines which was valued at ₱30,742.47.

RHU furnish a copy of the Inventory of COVID-19 vaccine report to the Accounting Office for the latter to further verify and thereafter take-up the verified balance in the Municipality’s books of accounts to adjust balances of both the inventory and donation accounts. 

Henceforth, both offices agreed to observe proper coordination in order to prevent similar omissions/ errors in their future operations considering that the vaccination is still a continued priority program in 2023.

Management, through the Municipal Accountant exert effort to retrieve the documents of unidentified payables or those without details in the subsidiary ledgers, and effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the accounts erroneously booked in the Accounts Payable account to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to adjust the books of account to recognize the remaining value/cost of the unused accountable forms.  

Municipal Accountant set-up the Inventory accounts in the books upon receipt of the purchased accountable forms and record the corresponding expense accounts upon issuance and submission of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued/Requisition Issue Slip pursuant to COA Circular No. 2002-003.

Municipal Mayor direct the contractor to undertake the repair works to correct the noted deficiencies on the project at their own expense, pursuant to Section 62 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and inform the Audit Team of the actions taken subject to re-evaluation.

Management carefully plan the Development Projects/Activities/Programs under the 20 per cent DF by conducting a thorough study/investigation of projects, survey of areas/location of the proposed projects to avoid problems with non-existence of identified site, and duplication of projects to be funded by other agencies prior to their inclusion in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan in order to materialize the same in the year these were projected/scheduled to be completed.

Management facilitate the timely preparation of Programs of Work and Estimates of the proposed projects and the timely procurement of the same.

Finally, we recommended that Management evaluate the viability of the projects that were not implemented during the year for appropriate realignment.

Management submit a written justification on the noted deficiency and revert the ₱20.950 million to the special account under the 20 per cent DF.

All PPAs which are no longer feasible and have not yet been implemented be subjected for immediate realignment, pursuant to budgetary rules or reverted back to the unappropriated surplus of the 20 per cent DF for re-appropriation to other development projects.
Management through the Chairperson of Local Peace and Order Council (LPOC) strictly adhere to the provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GC for GOCCs-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.
Management, through the LDRRMO, submit an explanation on the deficiencies noted and cause the submission of the revised LDRRM plan pursuant to the requirements provided in the JMC.

Management, through the SB enact a local ordinance creating the DRRM Office including the provision/allocation of budget and the required staffing/personnel. Thereafter, conduct the immediate hiring of qualified DRRM Officer and staff to fill-in the positions created pursuant to the IRR of RA No. 10121.

Municipal Mayor submit legal basis, if any, on the hiring of Job Order worker designated as Public Information Officer while the said JO is still the incumbent SK Chairperson of Barangay Lagunde to avoid the issuance of Notice of Disallowance for this transaction.

Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to release the RPT share to Barangays, on a quarterly basis, pursuant to Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 in order for the latter to utilize the funds for its priority projects.

Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to submit written justification on why the performance and warranty security were not required in the procurement of goods and the above-mentioned documents deemed as lacking.

Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC require the suppliers to post the performance security prior to the signing of the contract and submit a warranty security or ensure that retention money is adequately provided for before payment as prescribed under Section 39 and 62 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid sanction provided under the law.

Management submit an acceptable justification/ explanation on the noted deficiency, for the Audit Team’s evaluation to prevent disallowance. Henceforth, advise the BAC and the Technical Working Group to revisit the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 and other relevant GPPB issuances and strictly observe the prohibition against making reference to brand names or tailor-fitting of specifications.

Management, through the Sangguniang Bayan they will sufficiently appropriate the one per cent of the NTA for the implementation of PAPs of the LCPC in order to comply with the budgetary requirement and to fully achieve the purpose of the fund pursuant to RA 9344.

The Management exhaust all possible remedies to collect and to demand liquidation of CAs especially from those who were already separated from the service, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

The Municipal Engineer and other officials concerned to explain the non-imposition and collection of LD from the supplier.
  
Also, to recoup the overpayment, representing the un-imposed LD, from the supplier or from the person/s responsible for the non-computation of the LD.

The Management establish clear cut guidelines, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, on the grant of AICS such as the qualification of indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, which shall align with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019.

The Municipal Officials and employees concerned and the PRAISE Committee submit a written justification on the grant of monetary awards, and the documents mentioned as lacking, to prevent the issuance of an audit disallowance on this transaction.

The Municipal Accountant to submit the FS and Reports enumerated under Sections 2 and 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and its supporting schedules as well as a valid written justification on his repeated failure to submit on time the said reports to prevent the withholding of his salary pursuant to Section 111 (par. 2) of PD No. 1445.

The Municipal Accountant to record the relief goods costing ₱1.460 million under the Welfare Goods for Distribution and the medical supplies costing ₱2.348 million as Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory so as not to understate the Inventory account

The Accountant to immediately enforce settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2014 by sending demand letters to each accountable officer. Nevertheless, if these accountable officers still fail to comply, the Local Chief Executive may advise in writing the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to suspend the salaries of the officials and employees to comply settlement of their outstanding accounts.

However, if demand letters were already sent, please inform us of the immediate settlements undertaken by these accountable officers, especially those that were unliquidated for more than one year.

pursuant to the directives of Section 379 of R.A. No. 7160 and in accordance with the
procedures cited in COA Circular 89-296.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Chairperson of the
Inventory and Disposal Committee to send letter of demand to those AOs for the
production of the missing properties. Otherwise, if accountability/responsibility over
the missing PPE could not be pinpointed after investigation, authority for
derecognition thereof from the books of accounts may be requested from the COA as
prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to exhaust all possible remedies to collect
and to demand liquidation of CAs especially from those who were already separated
from the service, otherwise, request authority to write-off from COA, if warranted.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prospectively observe
proper recording and accounting for LDRRMF transactions under TF in order to
reflect the correct entries and to achieve the fair presentation of the FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Engineer and
other officials concerned to explain the non-imposition and collection of LD from the
supplier. Also, we recommended that Management recoup the overpayment,
representing the un-imposed LD, from the supplier or from the person/s responsible
for the non-computation of the LD.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
stop the practice of obligating prior year’s expenses against the current year’s
appropriation pursuant to Section 350 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 119 of P.D. No.
1445.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record as payables all the
unpaid obligations at the end of the year in order to avoid the recording of expenses
in the ensuing year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all contracts entered into by the
government contain the terms and conditions, including all the necessary information
as set forth in the rules and regulations on Supply and Property Management and
other applicable issuances.

We recommended and Management agreed that the official/s concerned submit a
written justification on the above noted deficiencies; and the documents mentioned
as lacking, to prevent the issuance of an audit disallowance on this transaction.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Officials and
employees concerned and the PRAISE Committee submit a written justification on
the grant of monetary awards, and the documents mentioned as lacking, to prevent
the issuance of an audit disallowance on this transaction.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit to the Audit Team the
FDA’s LTO as drug retailer/wholesaler/distributor for the above-mentioned supplier
in order to regularize this procurement transaction. Henceforth, we recommended
that Management strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the
procurement of goods/services including the related issuances of the GPPB to ensure
that contracts are awarded to eligible suppliers who have acquired the appropriate
licenses.

We recommended that Management establish clear cut guidelines, concurred by the
Sangguniang Bayan, on the grant of AICS such as the qualification of indigent, amount
and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements, and the like, which shall align
with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to
provide complete details of the information necessary in the signboard being posted
on the projects’ site pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004. Henceforth, strict
compliance is hereby enjoined.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and other
officials concerned submit written justification on why the Annual and Supplemental
Budgets for CY 2021 was not submitted to the SP for review. Henceforth, observe the
budget process, and comply with the requirements in accordance with the pertinent
law, rules, and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant properly
withhold taxes on the benefits paid to elected and appointed officials in excess of the
ceiling and remit the same to the BIR pursuant to the TRAIN law.

Record the corresponding expense upon issuance thereof.

Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory									2,348,477.75
Due to LGU														₱499,780.00

To record Inventory upon actual disbursements of the Financial Assistance from the Cebu PTO and LDRRM Fund which were directly debited to the “Due to LGU” account and “Trust Liability- LDRRMF”, respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator submit a valid/written justification on why the INFRA projects under the 20% Development Plan have not been implemented for CY 2020 nor realigned such that the fund could be utilized to undertake critical, urgent, and appropriate response aid and measures in a timely manner to curtail and eliminate the threat of COVID-19.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Budget Officer submit a written justification and legal basis in the granting of said benefits to the elected and appointed Barangay employees to prevent disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Budget Officer see to it that disbursements do not exceed the appropriated amounts to avoid future disallowance of the same nature.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the BAC and the non-BAC members concerned submit justification on the above noted deficiencies, to prevent disallowance of the irregular claim of traveling expenses totaling ₱127,247.75. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit explanation/justification on non-compliance with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020.
Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC should always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Local Sanggunian and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit a valid written justification on the noted deficiencies and make representation to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) on the mandatory posting requirement in the absence of the LGU’s Official Website.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a copy of an Appropriation Ordinance indicating the sources of funds available or appropriated duly certified under oath jointly by the local treasurer and the local accountant and attested to by the Local Chief Executive the items of appropriations affected and the reason for the change thereof, pursuant to the above provisions.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer submit a certified copy of the available funds or income actually collected indicating also the itemized class of expenditures and the fiscal year where the mentioned declared savings were attributed to support the submitted LBC Form 08 – Statement of Funding Sources as well as to determine the validity and propriety of expenditures incurred thereon.
Henceforth, strict compliance is hereby enjoined.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Bids and Awards Committee submit a valid written justification and the documents mentioned as lacking to prevent disallowance in audit.
We also recommended that the concerned officials refund the amount of ₱13,200.00 representing overpayment in the procurement of air purifier and irradiation lamp.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should check the accuracy of the amount to be paid and the completeness of the attached documents before payment is made to avoid occurrence of the same nature.

We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation in regards to the deficiencies observed and the lacking documents mentioned above. observations.recommendation: We recommend that all concerned officials be directed to refund the P2,000.00 excess amount of PEI received otherwise, all those involved who authorized and approved the payment thereof shall be held liable  therefore.
observations.managementAction: Officials and employees who separated from service were required to pay in full their total disallowances before they will be given their terminal leave pay.
observations.reason: Management had started its refunds thru payroll deduction and some were deducted only upon retirement/resignation from the government service.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend that the unused balances of fund transfers with completed projects or unutilized fund transfers due to non-implementation of projects need to be returned immediately to the source agencies in compliance to Section 4.10 of COA Circular 94-013. We likewise recommend for the return of the unutilized PDAF to the National Treasury so that these could be utilized for priority projects of the National Government. 

Likewise, instruct the Accountant to exert more efforts on how the very old unused balances could be returned to its source agencies and reclassify all accounts that were erroneously entered in Due to National Government Agencies or Due To NGAs account.
observations.managementAction: Management have liquidated some of these funds and are just waiting for the bills or request for Liquidations from source agencies.
observations.reason: The Municipality had returned some of the funds sourced from NGAs.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend that the Municipal Engineer’s Office prioritize its implementation of infrastructure projects and, despite personnel limitations, strategize so that infrastructure and other projects chargeable against the Development Fund could be implemented within the most reasonable time. 

However, Top Management (the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive) must find ways and means to solve the problem of lack of sufficient competent personnel in crucial departments, such as the MEO and MPDC, so that development plans by the LGU, especially the implementation thereof, would not be hindered.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer justified that they lacked personnel to fully implement Infra projects.
observations.reason: Management has implemented the said projects, however, there was still delay in the implementation of Infra projects like the various water systems of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should observe the rules and regulations governing the use of government motor transportations and effect the marking “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” three (3) inches in size and the corresponding name of Office on each side of the vehicle to prevent any unnecessary and unofficial use of government motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Management has marked all vehicles with the “For Official Use Only”, except for one vehicle (that which is assigned to the Mayor’s Office).
observations.reason: Ocular inspection of all motor vehicles disclosed that one (1) vehicle assigned to the Municipal Mayor’s Office had not been marked “For Official Use Only”.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned municipal drivers prepare and accomplish properly the pre-numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets (DTT) per vehicle indicating the important data mentioned above. Purchase of fuel on account especially by each driver should be supported with duly approved RIV/PR/PO and original Charge Invoice every time a purchase is made. The quantity purchased, fuel usage as well as ending balances should be recorded in the DTT to facilitate the audit of gasoline and oil consumption and the reasonableness of fuel   consumed for a particular period.
observations.managementAction: Management duly approved the RIV/PR/PO. The quantity purchased, fuel usage and ending balances are not recorded in Driver’s Trip Tickets.
observations.reason: Verification of the submitted DVs disclosed that there was partial compliance made by Management wherein necessary data were indicated in the DTT’s.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant immediately enforce settlement of all outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2014 by sending demand letters to each accountable officer. Nevertheless, if these accountable officers still fail to comply, the Local Chief Executive may advise in writing the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to suspend the salaries of the officials and employees to comply settlement of their outstanding accounts.   However, if demand letters were already sent, please inform us of the immediate settlements undertaken by these accountable officers, especially those that were unliquidated for more than one year.
observations.managementAction: Management had partly settled liquidations of cash advances.
observations.reason: Management personnel had started liquidating some of their cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management procure goods directly from suppliers based on canvass of at least three (3) suppliers for purchases that does not need public bidding and discourage to stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru cash advances or reimbursements but should be based on properly conducted canvass and by paying directly to suppliers to ascertain the validity and propriety thereof.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursement basis of procuring goods and services has been minimized.
observations.reason: Scrutiny of 2017 DVs showed that it is still practiced, esp. on the Mayor’s cash advances and reimbursements on socio-cultural activities, were noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property tax collection. The Municipality should avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that demand letters were sent to delinquent taxpayers in its intensive tax collection campaign.
observations.reason: Management partly complied with this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality should appropriate funds for the titling of lots owned. The Municipal Assessor should be directed to facilitate the titling of these lots in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Management cited that they have been coordinating with DENR in its “Public Land Titling” program, and the municipal assessor is the focal person on this matter.
observations.reason: Management is in the process of preparing and gathering the documents necessary for the titling of the lots.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with Sections 32 and 34 of the Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units. The Municipal Treasurer should deposit intact her collections as well as all collections turned over to her  by the collectors/tellers with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day to prevent possible loss or misuse of government  funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 | The Municipal Treasurer tried her best to deposit her collections intact in the bank.
observations.reason: Considerable distance between Oslob and its government depository banks made it difficult to deposit collections daily. However, it was noted that Land Bank of the Phils. had established a new branch in Dalaguete starting December 2015. Thus, the Treasurer is advised to transfer its account to the LBP-Dalaguete branch.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation to create an Inventory Committee to immediately conduct actual physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE in order to determine its existence and to render a report (Report on the Physical Count of PPEs or RPCPPE) thereon, a copy of which must be submitted to the auditor in compliance with Section 124, Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System. The Physical Inventory Report should be reconciled with accounting records.
observations.managementAction: 2013 AAR | Management have not conducted its inventory-taking and has yet to create an inventory committee.
observations.reason: Management was required to submit its Report of Actual Physical Count of its Property, Plant and Equipment but failed to produce a copy thereof.

observations.recommendation:
Unutilized Fund Transfers from various national government agencies, amounting to P20,599,959.71, were not covered by any MOA which would spell out the terms and conditions of the fund transfers,  completed projects were not liquidated and unused funds were not returned to the source agencies even if these were implemented already, contrary to Section 4 of  COA Circular  94-013.

We recommend for the submission of a copy of the MOA to the COA Audit Team for each fund transfer made by source agencies (national government agencies) and a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution for the Mayor to enter into such agreement and to execute such in accordance with the terms/conditions of the MOA and in compliance to government rules and regulations, such as COA Circular 94-013.  Likewise, for completed projects and completely utilized funds, the Local Chief Executive must compel the Accountant and Treasurer to return these unused funds to the source agencies upon submission of the fund utilization reports.
observations.managementAction: 2012 | Management has yet to locate  copies of MOAs for this projects.
observations.reason: Management has yet to submit copies of MOAs supporting the funds transferred from various NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management and other personnel concerned refund the amount of P319,142.82 expended during the conduct of election despite the prohibition mentioned under COA Circular No. 2013-004, otherwise, the officials who approved the payment thereof should be held liable therefor.

We recommend that the Local School Board limit the utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects specified under RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No.01 in order to maximize its implementation.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the above-cited transactions to its proper asset account by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the entries that were erroneously made, copy-furnished the COA for verification purposes.

Henceforth, major repairs which could enhance the life of a Property, Plant and Equipment should be capitalized. Likewise, procurement of supplies and materials should be taken up as “Inventories”. Expense account should only be used/recorded in the books based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued/ Inventory Custodian Slip.

To address the various observations noted:

•	The Municipal Treasurer must issue the Capital Seed Fund of P500,000.00 by check to the SEA-K associations (OslobPantawid SKA and GawiOslobPantawid SLPA) at P270,000 and P230,000, respectively, to comply with Section B.2.4-d of the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2014.  Otherwise, the entire transaction will be disallowed in audit. Only then will these associations release the individual loan, payable within two years, of  P10,000 each to its members. 

•	We also recommend that the Mayor instruct the Accountant to communicate with the DSWD Regional Accountant as to how the 

•	BUB Capital Assistance project (SEA-K mode) was taken up in its books so as to make the recording in the LGU books appropriate.

•	Also instruct the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to coordinate with the Accountant at all times in the submission of all documents necessary in the proper liquidation of all BUB projects and other fund transfers from the DSWD. 

Henceforth, to facilitate the audit and evaluation of Fund Utilization Reports (FUR), the  Municipal Accountant must submit the FUR of all fund transfers, which is to be supported completely with documents on the receipt of funds and actual utilization of such funds in accordance with the terms of the MOA and SIA as well as COA rules and regulations,  to COA for audit within ten days from which the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements has been submitted to the Accounting Section for recording or upon completion of the project as required under COA Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The P500,000.00 fund was refunded back to the Municipal Treasurer under O.R. # 9764350 dated June 6, 2016 and the same was released thru issued check no. 2877998 dated June 13, 2016 paid to OslobPantawid SKA, check no. 2877999 dated June 13, 2016 paid to Gawi, OslobPantawid SLPA worth P270,000.00 and P230,000.00 respectively.
observations.reason: Management had refunded the amount of P500,000.00 per verbal demand made.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommend to Management:

•	To stop immediately the procurement of cash tickets from private printing companies and turn over all unused cash tickets, records and cash to the Municipal Treasurer. These unused cash tickets shall be declared invalid and purchase immediately from the Provincial Government valid cash tickets and official receipts. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer shall procure cash tickets and ORs from the Provincial Office and account for all revenues and disburse all expenses relating to the tourist/whale-watching operations of the agency in accordance with COA rules and regulations.

•	The Mayor’s staff must immediately inventory all records on collections, including unused cash tickets and official receipts and all cash and accountability to the Municipal Treasurer and turn over so that these collections could be accounted for and disbursed in accordance with government rules and regulations. 

•	Once the immediate turn-over is effected, instruct the chief of the Deputized Collecting Officer in Tan-awan tourist operations to turn over to the Municipal Treasurer on a daily basis, duly receipted by her. Likewise, systematize the reporting of collections by the DBCs and distribution of sharing of daily collections to Tan-awanOslob Sea Wardens and Fisheries Association (60%), Municipality of Oslob (30%) and Barangay Tan-awan (10%).

•	Create and instruct a committee to institutionalize/establish the revenue-generating activities in Tan-awan and other tourist destinations in  Oslob, such as Nueva Caceres, Sumilon Island (Barangay Bangcogon) and La Caida de Agua/Tumalug Falls (Barangays Tumalug and Luka), into an economic enterprise concept so that it could be determined which locations or barangays are profitable or need subsidies/assistance.

observations.managementAction: 2011 AAR | Management cited that they have been coordinating with DENR in its “Public Land Titling” program, and the municipal assessor is the focal person on this matter.
observations.reason: Management is in the process of preparing and gathering the documents necessary for the titling of the lots.
